Web research APIs for AI: search, extrac: Web research APIs for AI: search, extract, multi-hop research tasks, entity discovery (FindAll), and web monitoring.
\n**Get your Parallel API key:**\n\n1. S:
**Get your Parallel API key:**

1. Sign in at https://platform.parallel.ai
2. Navigate to **API Keys**
3. Create a new key and paste it here.
Search the web with natural-language obj: Search the web with natural-language objectives. Returns LLM-optimized excerpts with citations.
Extract clean, citation-aware content fr: Extract clean, citation-aware content from specific web URLs. Up to 20 URLs per request.
Initiate a Parallel research task. Retur: Initiate a Parallel research task. Returns immediately with a queued run; use Get Task Run Result to wait for completion.
Retrieve the current status of a task ru: Retrieve the current status of a task run by its ID.
Retrieve a task run result by run ID. Bl: Retrieve a task run result by run ID. Blocks until the run completes (or until the timeout is reached).
Discover and verify entities matching pl: Discover and verify entities matching plain-language criteria. Match conditions can be supplied directly or auto-generated from the objective.
Return the current snapshot of matched c: Return the current snapshot of matched candidates for a FindAll run, including any enrichment fields.
Get a chat completion using a Parallel p: Get a chat completion using a Parallel processor. OpenAI-compatible interface; processor names like 'speed', 'base', 'core', 'pro', 'ultra' map to Parallel tiers.
Make a custom API call to a specific end: Make a custom API call to a specific endpoint
Natural-language description of the unde: Natural-language description of the underlying question or goal driving the search. Should be self-contained with enough context to understand the intent.
Concise keyword search queries (3-6 word: Concise keyword search queries (3-6 words each). At least one query is required; 2-3 is best.
Basic: low latency with 2-3 high-quality: Basic: low latency with 2-3 high-quality queries. Advanced: higher quality with deeper retrieval.
Upper bound on total characters across e: Upper bound on total characters across excerpts from all results.
Optional list of domains to restrict res: Optional list of domains to restrict results to (e.g. wikipedia.org, .gov).
Optional list of domains to exclude from: Optional list of domains to exclude from results.
Optional session identifier to share con: Optional session identifier to share context across search/extract calls.
List of URLs to extract content from (ma: List of URLs to extract content from (max 20).
Natural-language description of the goal: Natural-language description of the goal driving the extraction. Used to focus excerpts on the most relevant content.
Optional keyword search queries used tog: Optional keyword search queries used together with the objective to focus excerpts.
When enabled, requests full markdown of: When enabled, requests full markdown of each page in addition to excerpts. May increase latency.
Input to the task (free text or a JSON s: Input to the task (free text or a JSON string). Example: "What was the GDP of France in 2023?"
How the task should structure its output: How the task should structure its output.
Used when Output Schema Type is \"Text\": Used when Output Schema Type is "Text". Plain-language description of the desired output.
Used when Output Schema Type is \"JSON S: Used when Output Schema Type is "JSON Schema". A JSON Schema object describing the desired structured output.
Optional source policy: only return resu: Optional source policy: only return results from these domains.
Optional source policy: exclude results: Optional source policy: exclude results from these domains.
Optional key/value metadata stored with: Optional key/value metadata stored with the run (keys ≤16 chars, values ≤512 chars).
The task run ID returned from Create Tas: The task run ID returned from Create Task Run.
How long to wait for the run to complete: How long to wait for the run to complete. Defaults to 600 seconds.
Natural-language description of the targ: Natural-language description of the target entities. e.g. "AI companies that raised Series A funding in 2024".
Array of `{ \"name\": \"...\", \"descrip: Array of `{ "name": "...", "description": "..." }` objects describing the criteria each match must satisfy. Leave empty to auto-generate from the objective.
Generator tier — higher tiers use deeper: Generator tier — higher tiers use deeper search at higher cost.
Maximum number of matches to find (5–100: Maximum number of matches to find (5–1000). Defaults to 100.
Optional key/value metadata stored with: Optional key/value metadata stored with the run.
The FindAll run ID returned from Create: The FindAll run ID returned from Create FindAll Run.
Optional system message to set the assis: Optional system message to set the assistant behaviour.
Whether the model should return free tex: Whether the model should return free text or a JSON object.
Used when Response Format is \"JSON Sche: Used when Response Format is "JSON Schema". Provide a JSON Schema object.
Name for the JSON schema (required when: Name for the JSON schema (required when Response Format is JSON Schema).
Authorization headers are injected autom: Authorization headers are injected automatically from your connection.
Enable for files like PDFs, images, etc.
HEAD: HEAD
